summary: Independent QA accepts M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-EXIT-001 as prerequisite evidence only. The lead synthesis and systems ACCEPT truthfully carry all tracked M7 UI/localization/visual quality tasks as CLOSED, record the required build/test evidence, confine this branch to allowed process paths, and preserve Human Gate boundaries. Edge/Firefox remain blocked-by-tooling rows and Windows DPI 150% remains a follow-up row; none are represented as full platform coverage.

decisions.decision: Accept this task only as prerequisite evidence for later M7 content-lock and exit validation. It is not formal content lock approval, M7 Gate PASS, release readiness, production art/culture approval, or manual human acceptance.
Accept the lead evidence matrix: all tracked M7 UI/localization/visual quality rows are CLOSED, and the only material platform limitations are explicitly carried as unresolved/follow-up rows.

Accept Edge and Firefox only as blocked-by-tooling rows and Windows DPI 150% only as a follow-up row. Later M7 content-lock or exit claims must carry or resolve them before any full-platform-coverage statement.

No Human Gate is activated or satisfied by this review. Formal content lock, manual human acceptance, production art/cultural choices, release/commercial decisions, telemetry/accounts/secrets, cloud-save implementation, server/multiplayer, and manual node battle reversal remain outside this task.

Accept the branch diff scope. It is confined to the task JSON, thread registry, project/messages, goal-mode-state, and model-routing-state. No product code, dependencies, CI, provenance, content-source, gate docs, or release artifacts changed.

QA did not rerun build:web, storybook:build, full e2e, desktop security/package, or pnpm check because this branch is process-only. Acceptance relies on lead synthesis, validated local evidence files, accepted upstream reviewer handoffs, and upstream CI records for those heavy commands.

risks.summary: Edge and Firefox are not run in the current Playwright setup and remain blocked by tooling.
risks.mitigation: Carry into M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 and M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 unless browser projects/tooling are added and evidence rerun.

risks.summary: Windows DPI 150% is not directly exercised by current browser or desktop smoke tooling.
risks.mitigation: Carry as a follow-up row until a dedicated DPI harness or explicit accepted limitation exists.

risks.summary: Because this synthesis is close to M7 gate material, downstream closure could overclaim content lock, full platform coverage, release readiness, or human manual acceptance.
risks.mitigation: Lead closure must retain this QA ACCEPT as prerequisite evidence only and keep all Human Gate rows visible.

risks.summary: QA reran process/schema/diff/path checks and reviewed evidence, but did not rerun the heavy product commands on this process-only branch.
risks.mitigation: Use the recorded lead synthesis, accepted upstream task handoffs, and upstream CI as the source of truth for those heavy command results.
